description: Rules for distinguishing natural language from Python concept references in course quality audit

natural_language_suppression._note: Words in this list are ordinary Russian words that should not trigger premature concept detection when used in dialogue or mission text. They are either: (a) fundamental grammatical conjunctions used in everyday speech, or (b) general-purpose verbs/nouns that happen to overlap with Python concept names.

explicit_instruction_triggers._note: These phrases explicitly ask the student to use a Python concept. They SHOULD trigger detection even in dialogue context, because they are instructional, not conversational.

code_token_triggers._note: These are actual Python keyword tokens that indicate the concept is being used in code context. They should trigger detection even in dialogue when they appear as inline code or code examples.
